name: IDE and coding agents with MCP
current_usage_summary: IDE-embedded agents (Cursor, VS Code Copilot/agent modes, Windsurf) connect to MCP servers via project or global config. Users install servers from marketplaces or hand-edit mcp.json. The agent runtime discovers tools at session start (or via deferred loading where supported), selects tools by description match, and invokes them through JSON-RPC over stdio (local) or Streamable HTTP/SSE (remote). Configuration is declarative; the model rarely sees transport details—only tool names, descriptions, and JSON Schema parameters in the tool list.
typical_stack: Config: .cursor/mcp.json (project) merged with ~/.cursor/mcp.json (global). Servers: npx/@modelcontextprotocol/server-* binaries, Docker sidecars, or vendor HTTPS endpoints. Transports: stdio for local dev tools (filesystem, git, DB); HTTP for SaaS (Linear, Notion, cloud APIs). Agent loop: user prompt → planner → tool_call → MCP client → server → structured result → re-injected into chat. Ecosystem directories: cursor.directory, Claude connector directory, npm packages @modelcontextprotocol/*.
auth_and_identity_model: Local stdio servers inherit OS user identity and env vars (GITHUB_TOKEN, AWS_PROFILE)—ambient authority. Remote HTTP servers use OAuth 2.1 per MCP spec (2025-11-25): Protected Resource Metadata, authorization server discovery, PKCE, optional Dynamic Client Registration or Client ID Metadata Documents (CIMD). User completes browser/device flow once; client stores refresh tokens. Per-project MCP config can scope which servers load. No standard cross-IDE capability attenuation—access is all-or-nothing per connected server unless the host implements tool allowlists.
context_and_token_behavior: Naive setup loads every tool schema into the system prompt or tools channel—GitHub MCP cited at 44k–55k tokens vs hundreds for gh CLI. Multi-turn chats may re-send or partially repeat schemas. Large JSON tool outputs flow back into context unless the host truncates or uses programmatic patterns. Cursor/docs note agents auto-pick relevant tools but do not eliminate upfront schema cost unless tool search/defer_loading is enabled (Claude ecosystem pattern; adoption in IDEs varies).
operational_pain_points: M×N integrations when each agent host reimplements OAuth flows. Connection flakiness (stdio process death, HTTP timeouts). Version skew between server and client spec. Secret sprawl in env for local servers. Hard to audit which tool ran with which tenant in multi-user orgs. Ops teams lack uniform envelope for SIEM—logs differ per server implementation.
developer_experience_friction: Debugging opaque JSON-RPC failures. Writing MCP servers heavier than wrapping existing CLI. Schema design debates (one tool per API endpoint vs intent-grouped). Developers maintain parallel surfaces: MCP for agents, REST/CLI for humans. Marketplace discovery helps but quality uneven.
gax_mapping: Invocation plane: agent issues `gax <cmd>` instead of raw MCP tool names—schemas live in gaxd registry, exposed via `gax doc/search` only when needed. Control plane: gaxd holds OAuth broker + cap minting; replaces per-server token stores for wrapped commands. Data plane: envelope v1 with surface=model replaces ad hoc MCP content blobs. MCP server becomes adapter behind manifest (mcp_bridge adapter type in roadmap)—model never sees tools/list.
migration_phases: Phase A (shadow): Run gaxd locally; register MCP servers as adapters; compare envelopes vs native MCP for same tasks. Phase B (agent config): Point Cursor/IDE shell tool or custom MCP 'gax' shim to invoke gax CLI only. Phase C (policy): Mint caps per workflow; disable direct MCP in mcp.json except gax bridge. Phase D (hosted gaxd): Central registry + enterprise policy; IDE connects to org gaxd URL with SSO-issued caps.
adapter_or_bridge_strategy: MCP→GAX bridge process: gaxd maintains manifest entries mapping command ids to {mcp_server, tool_name, arg_map}. On invoke, bridge opens existing MCP session (stdio subprocess or HTTP), calls tools/call, normalizes result to envelope v1, applies projection. Incremental: wrap highest-token servers first (GitHub, Jira). OpenAPI manifest generator (roadmap) for HTTP-native services without MCP.
breaking_changes_and_mitigations: Breaks: tool names in prompts/skills referencing mcp__github__*. Mitigation: alias table in registry; deprecate aliases over releases. Breaks: OAuth re-login when moving tokens to vault. Mitigation: import existing refresh tokens into tenant vault. Breaks: interactive MCP Apps UI. Mitigation: keep thin MCP client for UI-only tools or elicitation passthrough until GAX supports elicitation extension.
infrastructure_changes: Add gaxd daemon (local systemd/launchd or k8s sidecar). Optional enterprise gaxd cluster with Redis for cap revocation, OPA bundle sync, OTel exporter to existing observability. CI: lint manifests, schema contract tests. No change to IDE binary—only mcp.json entries and agent rules.
deployment_topology: Solo dev: gaxd on localhost:9477 (prototype default), caps in env GAX_CAP. Team: shared gaxd in dev VPC; SSO at login. Enterprise: regional gaxd replicas, vault (HashiCorp/cloud SM), audit bus to Splunk/Datadog. IDE still local; invokes gax CLI → corporate gaxd.
adoption_drivers: 85%+ token reduction when combined with lazy discovery (aligned with MCP tool search economics). Single audit_id per action for compliance. One cap token for least-privilege vs full MCP server access. Simpler agent prompts: learn gax grammar once.
adoption_blockers: IDE vendors invested in native MCP UX (marketplace, MCP Apps). Developers already published MCP servers—sunk cost. Need host support for cap injection (today manual env). Network path: corporate proxy may block new daemon port.
adoption_timeline_estimate: Enthusiast solo devs: 6–12 months after stable gaxd + bridge; enterprise IDE fleets: 2–4 years aligned with security mandate cycles (similar to SSO for internal APIs).
historical_parallel: Kubernetes sidecars / service mesh: data plane stays familiar (HTTP), control plane centralizes policy—GAX mirrors with CLI-shaped invocation + gaxd policy.

primary_sources.title: Claude Code MCP tool search (lazy loading)
